channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Second Australian teen dies in tainted alcohol case in Laos that has killed 6 tourists
item.description: VANG VIENG, Laos (AP) — A second Australian teenager who fell critically ill after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos has died in a hospital in Bangkok, her family said Friday, bringing the death toll in the mass poisoning of foreign tourists to six.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Bahamas will refinance part of its debt to protect its famous turquoise waters
item.description: The Bahamas says it will refinance $300 million of its external debt to free up more than $120 million for marine conservation projects and climate change mitigation.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea says Russia supplied air defense missiles to North Korea in return for its troops
item.description: A top South Korean official says Russia has supplied air defense missiles to North Korea in exchange for it sending troops.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Many in Gaza are eating just once a day, as hunger spreads amid aid issues
item.description: Palestinian families in the Gaza Strip are often eating just once a day, even in areas where aid groups have relatively more access than the isolated and heavily destroyed north.
item.dc:creator: By Wafaa Shurafa And Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UN nuclear agency’s board condemns Iran for the 2nd time this year for failing to fully cooperate
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — The U.N. nuclear watchdog’s board on Thursday condemned Iran for failing to cooperate fully with the agency, the second time it has done so in just five months.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top war-crimes court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu and others in Israel-Hamas fighting
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The world’s top war-crimes court issued arrest warrants Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and Hamas’ military chief, accusing them of crimes against humanity in connection with the 13-month war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US briefed Ukraine ahead of Putin's 'experimental Intermediate-range ballistic' attack
item.description: The U.S. alerted Ukraine and other allies ahead of Russia's attack that Moscow might use this "experimental" ballistic weapon to help Kyiv prepare ahead of the attack on Dnipro, a U.S. official confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australian teen and British woman who drank tainted alcohol in Laos have died, bringing toll to 5
item.description: VIENTIANE, Laos (AP) — An Australian teenager and a British woman have died after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos in what Australia’s prime minister on Thursday said was every parent’s nightmare.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Treasury imposes sanctions on Russian banks, financial officials
item.description: Russia’s third-largest bank, Gazprombank, and its subsidiaries were hit with U.S. sanctions Thursday, making it the largest bank not already targeted as the Russia-Ukraine war nears the three-year mark.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Police in Finland arrest five suspects over separatist violence in southeast Nigeria
item.description: HELSINKI, Finland (AP) — Police in Finland said Thursday they had detained five suspects in connection with deadly violence in southeastern Nigeria and were seeking a court extension of the detentions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu office rips ICC after warrant issued
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's office said in a statement that Israel "utterly rejects" the International Criminal Court's (ICC) arrest warrants issued Thursday for Netanyahu, former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant and a senior Hamas official for allegations related to war crimes from the
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Russia fired ICBM at Ukraine, Kyiv says
item.description: Russian forces launched an intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) at Ukraine, according to Ukrainian officials in the military.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: International Criminal Court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu, Hamas leader
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has issued arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Mohammed Diab Ibrahim Al-Masri, a Hamas leader commonly known as Mohammed Deif who is presumed dead, according to separate press releases.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Ex-UK Deputy Prime Minister John Prescott is hailed as a working-class hero after his death at 86
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British politician John Prescott, a pugnacious and personable former merchant seaman who rose to the post of deputy prime minister in Tony Blair's Labour government, has died at age 86. Prescott’s family announced his death on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ursula Haverbeck, German far-right activist repeatedly convicted for Holocaust denial, dies at 96
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Ursula Haverbeck, a prominent German far-right activist who accumulated a string of convictions for denying the Holocaust, has died. She was 96. Haverbeck died on Wednesday, her lawyer Wolfram Nahrath told German news agency dpa on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 19 Senators back limiting offensive weapons to Israel in key vote
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday failed to pass three resolutions that would have limited offensive arms to Israel, but 19 senators supported the initiative, showing there is growing resistance to the Israeli war against Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Yaks, bears and cockatoos are among the animals Russia is sending to North Korean zoo
item.description: Russia has sent a gift of more than 70 animals to North Korea for the Pyongyang Zoo, including bears, yaks, ducks and cockatoos.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: About 1,500 migrants form a new caravan in Mexico. Here's what it means
item.description: TAPACHULA, Mexico (AP) — About 1,500 migrants formed a new caravan Wednesday in southern Mexico, hoping to walk or catch rides to the U.S. border. The migrants are mainly from Central and South America.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Brazil’s Lula welcomes China's Xi for state visit as ties between countries strengthen
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Wednesday welcomed China’s President Xi Jinping for a state visit at the Alvorada Palace in Brasilia, the latest sign of deepening ties between the two countries that analysts say may accelerate as Donald Trump returns to the Whi
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis rejects tradition, opts for humble wooden casket and burial outside Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis announced this week that he intends on having a much more simple funeral than those of his predecessors at the Vatican, including a humble wooden casket.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chief opponent to Uganda's president appears in court days after going missing
item.description: Kizza Besigye, a prominent critic of Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni, appeared in a military court Wednesday after being reported missing in Kenya.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu offers $5 million to Palestinians for every Israeli hostage they help free
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu offers a $5 million reward for any Palestinian in Gaza who helps secure the freedom of Israeli hostages still held by Hamas after more than a year in captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After 97 UN aid trucks in Gaza are ‘violently looted’ by armed men, food prices soar
item.description: Food prices are rising in Gaza following the robbery of a U.N. convoy over the weekend, during which, drivers were forced to unload aid at gunpoint, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Leader of Georgia's separatist region of Abkhazia resigns, ending unrest
item.description: Following days of unrest in which the opposition seized government buildings, the leader of Georgia's separatist region of Abkhazia has resigned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Son of Norway's crown princess arrested on suspicion of rape
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, the eldest son of Norway Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was arrested on suspicion of rape in Oslo late last night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US allies accuse Russia of 'escalating hybrid activities' against NATO, EU nations after data cables severed
item.description: U.S. allies accuse Russia of "escalating hybrid activities" against NATO and EU countries after two data cables were severed in the Baltic Sea this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World's hottest calendar drops 2025 edition, proceeds going to Hurricane Helene, Milton efforts
item.description: This year, the funds collected from calendar sales will support those impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton, to provide immediate relief and aid to impacted communities.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden's Ukraine missile move will have 'some impact': Retired major
item.description: Lyons questioned the timing of the decision, saying, “I’m not sure why we didn’t allow this two or three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Wreckage of World War II warship found 80 years later
item.description: The USS Edsall, a World War II American warship, was bombed and sank three months after the attack on Pearl Harbor.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Montana Tucker's Oct. 7 film showcases young victims' strength
item.description: Montana Tucker's film, "The Children of October 7," documents what some of the attacks' youngest victims went through.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation
